key_insight: A masked transformer autoencoder can reconstruct missing fetal heart rate segments while preserving temporal and spectral structure, enabling both inpainting and forecasting.
engineering_relevance: The method is directly relevant for wearable prenatal monitoring systems because it can make continuous FHR streams more usable despite signal dropouts from motion or sensor displacement.
method: Self-supervised masked transformer autoencoder learns from incomplete FHR time series.
Reconstructs missing intervals by capturing local temporal patterns and frequency components.
Supports both signal inpainting and short-horizon forecasting.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports robustness across varying missing-data durations, indicating the approach was evaluated for different gap lengths in continuous FHR episodes.
watch_item: Performance may degrade on highly irregular dropout patterns or when deployed on-device under strict latency and power constraints.
paper_title: FHRFormer: A Self-Supervised Masked Transformer Framework for Fetal Heart Rate Time-Series Inpainting and Forecasting
